app.name: LeishCheck
app.tagline:
Take care of your health.
Simple, fast and free.
app.disclaimer: This tool does not replace an in-person medical consultation.

consent.title: Your privacy matters
consent.scrollHint: Scroll to the end to continue
consent.checkbox: I have read and agree to the consent terms
consent.declineMessage: Without consent, screening cannot proceed. You can return anytime.
consent.alreadyAcceptedMessage: You accepted the consent terms on {{date}}. Your consent is valid for 90 days.
consent.term:
INFORMED CONSENT FORM

By continuing, you voluntarily agree to provide personal and health information for exclusive use in this Cutaneous Leishmaniasis risk screening tool.

DATA USED INCLUDES:
• Age, gender and approximate geographic location;
• Responses to the signs and symptoms questionnaire;
• Optional images of skin lesions (captured by the device camera).

PURPOSE:
This data will be used exclusively to calculate a risk estimate for Cutaneous Leishmaniasis and provide preliminary health guidance. This tool does NOT provide medical diagnosis.

PRIVACY AND SECURITY:
• Data is stored ONLY locally on your device (IndexedDB/localStorage).
• No personal data is sent to external servers.
• Captured images remain exclusively on your device.
• No data is shared with third parties.

YOUR RIGHTS (per Brazil's LGPD — Law 13.709/2018):
• You may revoke this consent at any time in the app settings.
• You may request deletion of your data by clearing the app data.
• You have the right to access, correct and delete your personal data.

DECLARATION:
I declare that I have read and understood the above information, and that I voluntarily agree to participate in the screening, aware that I may withdraw at any time without prejudice.

IMPORTANT:
• This application is an auxiliary screening tool and does NOT replace in-person medical consultation.
• If you suspect leishmaniasis, immediately seek a Basic Health Unit (UBS).
• Treatment for leishmaniasis is free through Brazil's Unified Health System (SUS).

questionnaire.title: Questionnaire
questionnaire.q1: Do you live in a rural or forested area?
questionnaire.q2: Have you traveled to areas with leishmaniasis cases?
questionnaire.q3: Do you have a skin lesion that won't heal?
questionnaire.q4: Is the lesion growing over time?
questionnaire.q5: Is the lesion painless and ulcerated?
questionnaire.q6: Have you had contact with infected animals?
questionnaire.q7: Do you live in an endemic area?
questionnaire.q9: Has the wound lasted more than 2 weeks?
questionnaire.q10: Did the lesion appear after an insect bite?

imageUpload.title: Lesion Photo
imageUpload.description: If possible, take a photo of the wound for better guidance. This step is optional.

result.emergencyMessage: Your signs are strongly suggestive. Seek a Basic Health Unit (UBS) urgently. Treatment is free through SUS.
result.lowDesc: Signs not very suggestive of cutaneous leishmaniasis.
result.lowOrientation: Low suspicion. Monitor and seek UBS if worsens.
result.mediumOrientation: Moderate signs. Medical consultation recommended soon.
result.highDesc: Signs strongly suggestive of cutaneous leishmaniasis.
result.highOrientation: Strongly suggestive signs. Seek UBS urgently.
result.disclaimerResult: Only a health professional can confirm the diagnosis.

education.title: Educational Material
education.mosquitoText: Cutaneous leishmaniasis is transmitted by the bite of a very small insect called the sand fly (Lutzomyia). It is smaller than a common mosquito and usually bites at dusk and at night. It lives in areas with dense vegetation, garbage, debris, and near domestic animals.
education.preventionText: To protect yourself: use repellent, install screens on windows and doors, avoid accumulation of garbage and debris in the yard, and keep domestic animals clean and protected.
education.phase1Desc: A small reddish bump appears on the skin, similar to an insect bite that doesn't improve. It may itch slightly.
education.phase2Desc: The bump grows and forms an open wound with raised edges. The center becomes deeper, like a crater. It usually doesn't hurt.
education.phase3Desc: Without treatment, the wound can grow significantly and last months or years. More than one wound may appear on different body parts.
education.warning: If you've identified a similar wound, seek a Basic Health Unit. Treatment is free through SUS.

install.title: Install LeishCheck
install.description: Quick access from your phone's home screen

ai.analysisFailed: AI analysis unavailable. Result based on questionnaire only.
ai.disclaimer: AI-assisted analysis — does not replace professional medical diagnosis.

audio.home: Welcome to LeishCheck. Take care of your health. Simple, fast and free. Tap Start Screening to begin.
audio.consent: Your privacy matters. Please read the consent form below and scroll to the end to continue.
audio.userData: Enter your basic information. All fields are optional. Tap Continue when you're ready.
audio.imageUpload: If you wish, take a photo of the lesion or select an image from the gallery. You can also skip this step.
audio.education: Educational material about cutaneous leishmaniasis. Here you'll find information about the sand fly and the phases of the disease.
